user_outcome: A SwornAgent CLI or automation operator can run `sworn board` from a project root without naming a release and receive a deterministic, source-attributed catalog of every valid local or remote-tracking release board, while named board queries retain their existing output shape.
rationale: Issue #123 exposed a missing project-level board oracle: callers could resolve a known release but could not discover the release set when records lived only on release assembly, track, or remote-tracking refs. This slice establishes one read-only catalog and makes the CLI its first consumer. S02 consumes the same selection contract for the TUI rather than implementing a second ref scan. C-01 is owned here.
in_scope: Add a read-only internal Git operation that enumerates available local heads and remote-tracking refs in bytewise lexical order, without invoking fetch, checkout, branch creation, ref update, worktree mutation, or a current-directory change. Ignore tags and remote symbolic-HEAD aliases because they are neither local heads nor remote-tracking release records.

Add one internal board-catalog resolver that examines only direct `docs/release/<release>/board.json` and legacy `docs/release/<release>/index.md` paths under each eligible ref and uses the existing BoardState/oracle path to validate the selected plan and resolve each slice's authoritative state. It must not read arbitrary working-tree paths or construct shell commands from ref or release values.

For every release name found on multiple candidates, select exactly one source ref using the ratified order: `refs/heads/release-wt/<release>`; then matching `refs/remotes/<remote>/release-wt/<release>` refs in bytewise lexical order; then other `refs/heads/*` candidates in bytewise lexical order; then other `refs/remotes/*` candidates in bytewise lexical order.

Treat a canonical local or remote release-worktree ref as authoritative evidence of that release even when it lacks a readable board. A missing record, malformed JSON, or board whose release identity does not match its direct release-directory name must stop the catalog with a deterministic error naming the release and ref; the command must not silently omit, substitute, or emit a partial successful catalog.

Make `--release` an optional board-command filter. With no filter, emit the C-01 aggregate JSON shape and readable, release-name-sorted sections that identify the selected source ref. With a filter, resolve through the same selection policy but retain the current single-release text and `{release,tracks}` JSON result without a `releases` wrapper or `sourceRef` field.

Preserve the existing track-branch, release-worktree, and selected-ref state priority inside each BoardState. A newer owning track-branch status must still win over the selected board plan's copied status.

Add real temporary-Git-repository integration fixtures that prove discovery works when HEAD contains no release board, including both canonical and noncanonical non-HEAD refs and a newer track-branch status. The fixture must execute the compiled `sworn` command from the consumer-project root, not invoke a catalog leaf directly.

Record in the implementation proof bundle the required mutation evidence: remove non-HEAD discovery or alter the source-rank ordering, observe the all-ref CLI reachability test fail, restore the implementation, and record the restored green run.
out_of_scope: TUI release-list and board-view loading changes are S02-tui-ref-aware-release-navigation.
Changing the release selection rules of route, merge, run, lint, or other named-release commands is outside #123 and remains deferred in the intake.
Fetching refs, configuring remotes, or inspecting tag-only release records is excluded; discovery operates only on refs already present in the local object database.
Changing the existing BoardState track/slice JSON schema is excluded; only the no-filter CLI envelope adds the C-01 catalog shape.

effort_complexity.rationale: The user-visible CLI behaviour is bounded, but correct all-ref discovery crosses the Git wrapper, board oracle, duplicate selection, failure handling, and a real Git integration fixture. Incorrect priority or a partial error path can silently choose the wrong release plan, so the blast radius is high.

risks.description: Different callers reimplement ref enumeration or selection and later expose inconsistent release catalogs.
risks.mitigation: Create one catalog resolver as the selection authority; both the no-filter CLI path and S02 consume it. C-01 and sourceRef assertions make divergence visible.

risks.description: A malformed or missing canonical release-worktree record is hidden by a valid lower-priority copy, causing automation to operate on stale planning state.
risks.mitigation: Canonical skew is an all-or-nothing error that names the ref and release. Fixtures cover missing, malformed, and identity-mismatched canonical records.

risks.description: Ref enumeration changes the caller's checkout or performs a network fetch, violating the operator's read-only expectation.
risks.mitigation: Use only read-only Git subprocesses with explicit argument vectors and snapshot the fixture's HEAD, branch, refs, porcelain status, and process working directory before and after the CLI command.

risks.description: The aggregate board reports a plan's stale release-worktree status even though the owner track has a newer status record.
risks.mitigation: Build the aggregate entries through the existing Oracle ReadBoard path and assert a newer track-branch slice status in the reachability fixture.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN `sworn board --json` runs from a temporary consumer Git repository whose checked-out HEAD has no `docs/release` board, two release boards exist only on non-HEAD refs, and one selected board has a newer owning `refs/heads/track/<release>/<track>` status record, THE SYSTEM SHALL exit 0 and emit exactly one JSON object with a `releases` object containing both release-name keys in bytewise lexical order. Each entry SHALL contain its matching `release`, its fully qualified selected `sourceRef`, and the existing `tracks` projection; the track-owned slice SHALL report the newer authoritative state rather than the stale selected-plan copy (C-01, N-01, N-04).

WHEN one release has valid direct board records on a local canonical release-worktree ref, two remote-tracking canonical release-worktree refs, a local noncanonical head, and a remote noncanonical ref, THE SYSTEM SHALL select `refs/heads/release-wt/<release>`. WHEN each higher class is removed in turn, it SHALL select the first remaining remote canonical ref in bytewise lexical order, then the first local noncanonical head in bytewise lexical order, then the first remote noncanonical ref in bytewise lexical order. `sourceRef` SHALL equal the selected fully qualified ref on every run (C-01, N-02).

WHEN an eligible canonical `release-wt/<release>` ref exists but has no board record, malformed board JSON, or a board release identity different from `<release>`, `sworn board --json` SHALL write no successful aggregate JSON, exit 2, and write a deterministic stderr error naming the release and fully qualified ref. It SHALL not omit that release, select a lower-priority copy, or retain a partial catalog. The same failure behaviour SHALL apply whether the canonical ref is local or remote-tracking (N-02, N-04).

WHEN `sworn board --release <release> [--json]` runs for a valid discovered release, THE SYSTEM SHALL use the same selected source-ref policy as the catalog while retaining the current single-release output contract: JSON contains top-level `release` and `tracks`, contains neither `releases` nor `sourceRef`, and text retains one existing release-board section. Existing named-release CLI tests and an explicit shape-regression assertion SHALL pass unchanged (C-01, N-04).

WHEN the all-ref CLI reachability fixture snapshots its Git HEAD, current branch, sorted refs, porcelain status, and process working directory immediately before and after `sworn board --json`, THE SYSTEM SHALL leave every snapshot byte-identical. `go test ./internal/git ./internal/board ./cmd/sworn`, `go test ./...`, and `go vet ./...` SHALL exit 0, and `gofmt -l .` SHALL produce empty output. The S01 proof bundle SHALL include a transcript showing the all-ref reachability test fail after temporarily removing non-HEAD discovery or changing the rank order, then pass after restoration (N-04).
